evals.prompt: I've added a new `--verbose` flag to the CLI. Commit this change.
evals.expected_output: A git commit with a concise imperative subject line (e.g. 'Add --verbose flag to CLI') and no unnecessary body.
assertions: Commit subject line uses imperative mood (Add/Fix/Update/Remove, not Added/Fixed)
Only relevant files are staged before committing
git status is checked before committing

evals.prompt: I fixed a bug where the login form wasn't clearing the password field after a failed attempt. Please commit.
evals.expected_output: A git commit with a subject line like 'Fix login form not clearing password on failed attempt'.
assertions: Commit subject line uses imperative mood
Subject line accurately describes the bug fix
No secrets or unrelated files are staged

evals.prompt: Commit everything that's changed. I've been refactoring the auth module.
evals.expected_output: The agent checks git status and git diff, then stages and commits only the auth-related changes with an appropriate message. It does not blindly stage all files without review.
assertions: git status is inspected before staging
git diff is reviewed before committing
Commit message reflects the refactor scope
Agent does not use 'git add .' without reviewing what would be staged
